name: Laguna de Pitillas and Roman City of Cara Loop
name_original: Laguna de Pitillas y ciudad romana de Cara – variante circular entre sotos y cultivos (BTT Zona Media)
description: This moderate 22.5-mile mountain biking route explores the Laguna de Pitillas Nature Reserve and the Roman City of Cara in Navarra.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Mélida offers diverse terrain, characterized by the semi-desert landscapes of the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. This region features unique rock formations, gorges, and plateaus, providing a distinctive backdrop for rides. Additionally, the area includes the protected wetlands of the Laguna de Pitillas Nature Reserve, adding varied ecosystems to explore. The terrain is well-suited for cross-country style mountain biking, with a mix of dirt roads and paths. Best mountain bike trails around Mélida The most popular mountain bike trail is Laguna de Pitillas and Roman City of Cara Loop, a 22.4 miles (36.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores both the natural reserve and historical sites. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Statue of the Bardenero Shepherd – Monument to the Bardenas Shepherd loop from El Paso, a moderate 18.1 miles (29.1 km) path. This route winds through the distinctive semi-desert landscape of Bardenas Reales. Local mountain bikers also love the Paso de los Ciervos – Statue of the Bardenero Shepherd loop from El Paso, a 22.0 miles (35.5 km) trail leading through the dramatic rock formations of the Bardenas, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around Mélida is defined by semi-desert landscapes, unique rock formations, and wetlands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Mélida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Mélida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Mélida?
A: There are 29 mountain bike trails around Mélida, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These include 7 easy, 14 moderate, and 8 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Mélida?
A: The terrain around Mélida is diverse, ranging from the unique semi-desert landscapes of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1170992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardenas Reales Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, with its distinctive rock formations and gorges, to the protected wetlands of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4047253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Pitillas Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. You'll find a mix of dirt roads and paths, well-suited for cross-country style mountain biking.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Mélida?
A: Yes, Mélida offers 7 easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes provide a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 8 difficult mountain bike routes around Mélida. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19885807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paso de los Ciervos – Statue of the Bardenero Shepherd loop from El Paso&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 22.0 miles with significant elevation changes, offer a demanding and rewarding experience through the dramatic Bardenas landscape.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while mountain biking in Mélida?
A: Many routes allow you to explore significant natural attractions. You can ride through the extraordinary semi-desert landscape of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1170992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardenas Reales Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique rock formations, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4047253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Pitillas Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, a protected wetland popular for birdwatching. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988423455/laguna-de-pitillas-and-roman-city-of-cara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Pitillas and Roman City of Cara Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience both.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988423455/laguna-de-pitillas-and-roman-city-of-cara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Pitillas and Roman City of Cara Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores both natural reserves and historical sites, including the Roman City of Cara. You might also encounter landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Bardenas Shepherd&lt;/a&gt; on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31817354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of the Bardenero Shepherd – Monument to the Bardenas Shepherd loop from El Paso&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes with good viewpoints in the Mélida area?
A: Yes, the region offers several viewpoints, especially within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Pass&lt;/a&gt; and other elevated sections provide panoramic views of the distinctive semi-desert landscape. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Pass – Castildetierra loop from El Paso&lt;/a&gt; are excellent for scenic vistas.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails around Mélida are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988423455/laguna-de-pitillas-and-roman-city-of-cara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Pitillas and Roman City of Cara Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40386610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of the Bardenero Shepherd – Paso de los Ciervos loop from Lo de la Reina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Mélida?
A: The Bardenas Reales Natural Park can experience extreme temperatures. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking, with milder temperatures and comfortable conditions for exploring the trails. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cold.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for every trail, the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, a primary mountain biking area, is well-developed for visitors, suggesting designated parking areas are available at key access points. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Mélida?
A: The mountain bike trails around Mélida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders frequently praise the unique and breathtaking semi-desert landscapes of the Bardenas Reales, the variety of terrain, and the well-maintained paths that offer both scenic beauty and engaging challenges.
tips: Small typical village of the Navarrese interior located on the banks of the Zidakos river and a few kilometers from the Laguna de Pitillas. It stands out for its stone buildings, picturesque streets and the Church of Santa Fé and the Hermitage of Santa Cruz with beautiful views of the valley.

The Laguna de Pitillas is located just 3 km from the town of the same name. It is a protected wetland of 216 hectares that has become and popularized among lovers of bird watching. Here you can see from bitterns, harriers and herons as well as the native vegetation of such a particular natural space.

The ruins of the ancient Roman city of Cara (Santacara) that can be visited today were built on an ancient Iron Age town estimated to date back to the 2nd century BC, making it one of the oldest ruins discovered in the area and that since 1993 have been protected under the figure of Asset of Cultural Interest.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate for mountain biking, corresponding to an STS S2 difficulty level. It requires a good level of physical fitness and some cycling experience. While not for absolute beginners, it's generally accessible for most skill levels with a decent fitness base.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail, named 'entre sotos y cultivos' (among groves and cultivated fields), features varied rural landscapes. You'll ride through agricultural areas, natural vegetation, and possibly riverine environments. Expect a mix of well-maintained paths as it's part of the BTT Zona Media network.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers a blend of nature and history. Key highlights include the tranquil Laguna de Pitillas Nature Reserve, a prominent natural feature, and the archaeological remains of the Roman City of Cara. You'll also pass through the settlement of Murillo el Cuende.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, the varied landscapes of Navarra, including cultivated fields and natural reserves, suggest that spring and autumn would offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route within the BTT Zona Media network. While specific parking locations aren't detailed, routes in this network typically have designated parking areas near their starting points, often in or near local villages like Murillo el Cuende. Look for signs indicating BTT trail access.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given that the trail passes through the Laguna de Pitillas Nature Reserve, it's crucial to check local regulations regarding dogs. Generally, dogs are allowed on trails but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users and livestock in agricultural areas.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Laguna de Pitillas Nature Reserve?
faqs.answer: Typically, access to natural reserves in Spain for activities like mountain biking does not require a specific permit or entrance fee, especially for designated trails. However, always respect signage and local regulations within the Laguna de Pitillas Nature Reserve to protect its delicate ecosystem.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 36.1 km circular route is approximately 2 hours and 38 minutes. This is an average estimate and can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the sights.

faqs.question: Are there any services like accommodation or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: As part of the 'BTT Zona Media' network in Navarra, this trail benefits from being in an area that promotes sustainable outdoor tourism. This often means access to local services. You can expect to find accommodation and restaurants in nearby towns and villages, such as Murillo el Cuende, which is along the route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: For this moderate mountain bike trail, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit, and appropriate cycling gear. Given the varied terrain and potential exposure to sun in cultivated fields, sunscreen, sunglasses, and a helmet are also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning you will start and end at the same point without having to retrace your path. This makes for convenient planning and a continuously changing landscape experience.
